root_text.an11.17:1.1: “Ekādasahi, bhikkhave, aṅgehi samannāgato gopālako abhabbo gogaṇaṁ pariharituṁ phātiṁ kātuṁ.
root_text.an11.17:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, gopālako na rūpaññū hoti, na lakkhaṇakusalo hoti, na āsāṭikaṁ hāretā hoti, na vaṇaṁ paṭicchādetā hoti, na dhūmaṁ kattā hoti, na titthaṁ jānāti, na pītaṁ jānāti, na vīthiṁ jānāti, na gocarakusalo hoti, anavasesadohī ca hoti, ye te usabhā gopitaro gopariṇāyakā te na atirekapūjāya pūjetā hoti.
root_text.an11.17:1.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, ekādasahi aṅgehi samannāgato gopālako abhabbo gogaṇaṁ pariharituṁ phātiṁ kātuṁ.
root_text.an11.17:2.1: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, ekādasahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu abhabbo imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjituṁ.
root_text.an11.17:2.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na rūpaññū hoti, na lakkhaṇakusalo hoti, na āsāṭikaṁ hāretā hoti, na vaṇaṁ paṭicchādetā hoti, na dhūmaṁ kattā hoti, na titthaṁ jānāti, na pītaṁ jānāti, na vīthiṁ jānāti, na gocarakusalo hoti, anavasesadohī ca hoti, ye te bhikkhū therā rattaññū cirapabbajitā saṅghapitaro saṅghapariṇāyakā te na atirekapūjāya pūjetā hoti.
root_text.an11.17:3.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na rūpaññū hoti?
root_text.an11.17:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu yaṁ kiñci rūpaṁ (…) ‘cattāri mahābhūtāni, catunnañca mahābhūtānaṁ upādāyarūpan’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
root_text.an11.17:3.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na rūpaññū hoti.
root_text.an11.17:4.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na lakkhaṇakusalo hoti?
root_text.an11.17:4.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu ‘kammalakkhaṇo bālo, kammalakkhaṇo paṇḍito’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
root_text.an11.17:4.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na lakkhaṇakusalo hoti.
root_text.an11.17:5.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na āsāṭikaṁ hāretā hoti?
root_text.an11.17:5.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu uppannaṁ kāmavitakkaṁ adhivāseti nappajahati na vinodeti na byantīkaroti na anabhāvaṁ gameti, uppannaṁ byāpādavitakkaṁ … uppannaṁ vihiṁsāvitakkaṁ …
root_text.an11.17:5.3: uppannuppanne pāpake akusale dhamme adhivāseti nappajahati na vinodeti na byantīkaroti na anabhāvaṁ gameti.
root_text.an11.17:5.4: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na āsāṭikaṁ hāretā hoti.
root_text.an11.17:6.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na vaṇaṁ paṭicchādetā hoti?
root_text.an11.17:6.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu cakkhunā rūpaṁ disvā nimittaggāhī hoti anubyañjanaggāhī;
root_text.an11.17:6.3: yatvādhikaraṇamenaṁ cakkhundriyaṁ asaṁvutaṁ viharantaṁ abhijjhādomanassā pāpakā akusalā dhammā anvāssaveyyuṁ, tassa saṁvarāya na paṭipajjati; na rakkhati cakkhundriyaṁ, cakkhundriye saṁvaraṁ nāpajjati.
root_text.an11.17:6.8: manasā dhammaṁ viññāya nimittaggāhī hoti anubyañjanaggāhī;
root_text.an11.17:6.9: yatvādhikaraṇamenaṁ manindriyaṁ asaṁvutaṁ viharantaṁ abhijjhādomanassā pāpakā akusalā dhammā anvāssaveyyuṁ, tassa saṁvarāya na paṭipajjati; na rakkhati manindriyaṁ, manindriye saṁvaraṁ nāpajjati.
root_text.an11.17:6.10: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na vaṇaṁ paṭicchādetā hoti.
root_text.an11.17:7.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na dhūmaṁ kattā hoti?
root_text.an11.17:7.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na yathāsutaṁ yathāpariyattaṁ dhammaṁ vitthārena paresaṁ desetā hoti.
root_text.an11.17:7.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na dhūmaṁ kattā hoti.
root_text.an11.17:8.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na titthaṁ jānāti?
root_text.an11.17:8.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu ye te bhikkhū bahussutā āgatāgamā dhammadharā vinayadharā mātikādharā, te kālena kālaṁ upasaṅkamitvā na paripucchati na paripañhati:
root_text.an11.17:8.4: Tassa te āyasmanto avivaṭañceva na vivaranti, anuttānīkatañca na uttānīkaronti, anekavihitesu ca kaṅkhāṭhāniyesu dhammesu kaṅkhaṁ na paṭivinodenti.
root_text.an11.17:8.5: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na titthaṁ jānāti.
root_text.an11.17:9.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na pītaṁ jānāti?
root_text.an11.17:9.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye desiyamāne na labhati atthavedaṁ, na labhati dhammavedaṁ, na labhati dhammūpasaṁhitaṁ pāmojjaṁ.
root_text.an11.17:9.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na pītaṁ jānāti.
root_text.an11.17:10.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na vīthiṁ jānāti?
root_text.an11.17:10.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
root_text.an11.17:10.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na vīthiṁ jānāti.
root_text.an11.17:11.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na gocarakusalo hoti?
root_text.an11.17:11.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu cattāro satipaṭṭhāne yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
root_text.an11.17:11.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na gocarakusalo hoti.
root_text.an11.17:12.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhuṁ saddhā gahapatikā abhihaṭṭhuṁ pavārenti cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānapaccayabhesajjaparikkhārehi. Tatra bhikkhu mattaṁ na jānāti paṭiggahaṇāya.
root_text.an11.17:12.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu anavasesadohī hoti.
root_text.an11.17:13.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu ye te bhikkhū therā rattaññū cirapabbajitā saṅghapitaro saṅghapariṇāyakā, te na atirekapūjāya pūjetā hoti?
root_text.an11.17:13.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu ye te bhikkhū therā rattaññū cirapabbajitā saṅghapitaro saṅghapariṇāyakā, tesu na mettaṁ kāyakammaṁ paccupaṭṭhāpeti āvi ceva raho ca, na mettaṁ vacīkammaṁ … na mettaṁ manokammaṁ paccupaṭṭhāpeti āvi ceva raho ca.
root_text.an11.17:13.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu ye te bhikkhū therā rattaññū cirapabbajitā saṅghapitaro saṅghapariṇāyakā, na te atirekapūjāya pūjetā hoti.
root_text.an11.17:14.1: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, ekādasahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu abhabbo imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjituṁ.
root_text.an11.17:15.1: Ekādasahi, bhikkhave, aṅgehi samannāgato gopālako bhabbo gogaṇaṁ pariharituṁ phātiṁ kātuṁ.
root_text.an11.17:15.3: Idha, bhikkhave, gopālako rūpaññū hoti, lakkhaṇakusalo hoti, āsāṭikaṁ hāretā hoti, vaṇaṁ paṭicchādetā hoti, dhūmaṁ kattā hoti, titthaṁ jānāti, pītaṁ jānāti, vīthiṁ jānāti, gocarakusalo hoti, sāvasesadohī ca hoti, ye te usabhā gopitaro gopariṇāyakā te atirekapūjāya pūjetā hoti—
root_text.an11.17:15.4: imehi kho, bhikkhave, ekādasahi aṅgehi samannāgato gopālako bhabbo gogaṇaṁ pariharituṁ phātiṁ kātuṁ.
root_text.an11.17:16.1: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, ekādasahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu bhabbo imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjituṁ.
root_text.an11.17:16.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu rūpaññū hoti, lakkhaṇakusalo hoti, āsāṭikaṁ hāretā hoti, vaṇaṁ paṭicchādetā hoti, dhūmaṁ kattā hoti, titthaṁ jānāti, pītaṁ jānāti, vīthiṁ jānāti, gocarakusalo hoti, sāvasesadohī ca hoti, ye te bhikkhū therā rattaññū cirapabbajitā saṅghapitaro saṅghapariṇāyakā te atirekapūjāya pūjetā hoti.
root_text.an11.17:17.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu yaṁ kiñci rūpaṁ ‘cattāri mahābhūtāni, catunnañca mahābhūtānaṁ upādāyarūpan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an11.17:17.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu rūpaññū hoti.
root_text.an11.17:18.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu ‘kammalakkhaṇo bālo, kammalakkhaṇo paṇḍito’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an11.17:18.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu lakkhaṇakusalo hoti.
root_text.an11.17:19.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu āsāṭikaṁ hāretā hoti?
root_text.an11.17:19.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu uppannaṁ kāmavitakkaṁ nādhivāseti pajahati vinodeti byantīkaroti anabhāvaṁ gameti, uppannaṁ byāpādavitakkaṁ … uppannaṁ vihiṁsāvitakkaṁ … uppannuppanne pāpake akusale dhamme nādhivāseti pajahati vinodeti byantīkaroti anabhāvaṁ gameti.
root_text.an11.17:19.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu āsāṭikaṁ hāretā hoti.
root_text.an11.17:20.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu vaṇaṁ paṭicchādetā hoti?
root_text.an11.17:20.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu cakkhunā rūpaṁ disvā na nimittaggāhī hoti nānubyañjanaggāhī;
root_text.an11.17:20.3: yatvādhikaraṇamenaṁ cakkhundriyaṁ asaṁvutaṁ viharantaṁ abhijjhādomanassā pāpakā akusalā dhammā anvāssaveyyuṁ, tassa saṁvarāya paṭipajjati; rakkhati cakkhundriyaṁ, cakkhundriye saṁvaraṁ āpajjati.
root_text.an11.17:20.8: manasā dhammaṁ viññāya na nimittaggāhī hoti nānubyañjanaggāhī;
root_text.an11.17:20.9: yatvādhikaraṇamenaṁ manindriyaṁ asaṁvutaṁ viharantaṁ abhijjhādomanassā pāpakā akusalā dhammā anvāssaveyyuṁ, tassa saṁvarāya paṭipajjati; rakkhati manindriyaṁ, manindriye saṁvaraṁ āpajjati.
root_text.an11.17:20.10: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu vaṇaṁ paṭicchādetā hoti.
root_text.an11.17:21.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu dhūmaṁ kattā hoti?
root_text.an11.17:21.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu yathāsutaṁ yathāpariyattaṁ dhammaṁ vitthārena paresaṁ desetā hoti.
root_text.an11.17:21.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu dhūmaṁ kattā hoti.
root_text.an11.17:22.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu ye te bhikkhū bahussutā āgatāgamā dhammadharā vinayadharā mātikādharā, te kālena kālaṁ upasaṅkamitvā paripucchati paripañhati:
root_text.an11.17:22.4: Tassa te āyasmanto avivaṭañceva vivaranti, anuttānīkatañca uttānīkaronti, anekavihitesu ca kaṅkhāṭhāniyesu dhammesu kaṅkhaṁ paṭivinodenti.
root_text.an11.17:22.5: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu titthaṁ jānāti.
root_text.an11.17:23.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye desiyamāne labhati atthavedaṁ, labhati dhammavedaṁ, labhati dhammūpasaṁhitaṁ pāmojjaṁ.
root_text.an11.17:23.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu pītaṁ jānāti.
root_text.an11.17:24.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an11.17:24.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu vīthiṁ jānāti.
root_text.an11.17:25.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu cattāro satipaṭṭhāne yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an11.17:25.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu gocarakusalo hoti.
root_text.an11.17:26.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu saddhā gahapatikā abhihaṭṭhuṁ pavārenti cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānapaccayabhesajjaparikkhārehi.
root_text.an11.17:26.4: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sāvasesadohī hoti.
root_text.an11.17:27.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu ye te bhikkhū therā rattaññū cirapabbajitā saṅghapitaro saṅghapariṇāyakā, te atirekapūjāya pūjetā hoti?
root_text.an11.17:27.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu ye te therā rattaññū cirapabbajitā saṅghapitaro saṅghapariṇāyakā, tesu mettaṁ kāyakammaṁ paccupaṭṭhāpeti āvi ceva raho ca, mettaṁ vacīkammaṁ … mettaṁ manokammaṁ paccupaṭṭhāpeti āvi ceva raho ca.
root_text.an11.17:27.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu ye te bhikkhū therā rattaññū cirapabbajitā saṅghapitaro saṅghapariṇāyakā, te atirekapūjāya pūjetā hoti.
root_text.an11.17:28.1: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, ekādasahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu bhabbo imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjitun”ti.

translation_text.an11.17:1.1: “Mendicants, a cowherd with eleven factors can’t maintain and expand a herd of cattle.
translation_text.an11.17:1.3: It’s when a cowherd doesn’t know form, is unskilled in characteristics, doesn’t pick out flies’ eggs, doesn’t dress wounds, doesn’t spread smoke, doesn’t know the ford, doesn’t know satisfaction, doesn’t know the trail, is not skilled in pastures, milks dry, and doesn’t show extra respect to the bulls who are fathers and captains of the herd.
translation_text.an11.17:1.4: A cowherd with these eleven factors can’t maintain and expand a herd of cattle.
translation_text.an11.17:2.1: In the same way, a mendicant with eleven qualities can’t achieve growth, improvement, or maturity in this teaching and training.
translation_text.an11.17:2.3: It’s when a mendicant doesn’t know form, is unskilled in characteristics, doesn’t pick out flies’ eggs, doesn’t dress wounds, doesn’t spread smoke, doesn’t know the ford, doesn’t know satisfaction, doesn’t know the trail, is not skilled in pastures, milks dry, and doesn’t show extra respect to senior mendicants of long standing, long gone forth, fathers and leaders of the Saṅgha.
translation_text.an11.17:3.1: And how does a mendicant not know form?
translation_text.an11.17:3.2: It’s when a mendicant doesn’t truly understand that all form is the four principal states, or form derived from the four principal states.
translation_text.an11.17:3.3: That’s how a mendicant doesn’t know form.
translation_text.an11.17:4.1: And how is a mendicant not skilled in characteristics?
translation_text.an11.17:4.2: It’s when a mendicant doesn’t understand that a fool is characterized by their deeds, and an astute person is characterized by their deeds.
translation_text.an11.17:4.3: That’s how a mendicant isn’t skilled in characteristics.
translation_text.an11.17:5.1: And how does a mendicant not pick out flies’ eggs?
translation_text.an11.17:5.2: It’s when a mendicant tolerates a sensual, malicious, or cruel thought that has arisen. They don’t give it up, get rid of it, eliminate it, and obliterate it.
translation_text.an11.17:5.3: They tolerate any bad, unskillful qualities that have arisen. They don’t give them up, get rid of them, eliminate them, and obliterate them.
translation_text.an11.17:5.4: That’s how a mendicant doesn’t pick out flies’ eggs.
translation_text.an11.17:6.1: And how does a mendicant not dress wounds?
translation_text.an11.17:6.2: When a mendicant sees a sight with their eyes, they get caught up in the features and details.
translation_text.an11.17:6.3: Since the faculty of sight is left unrestrained, bad unskillful qualities of covetousness and displeasure become overwhelming. They don’t practice restraint, they don’t protect the faculty of sight, and they don’t achieve its restraint.
translation_text.an11.17:6.4: When they hear a sound with their ears …
translation_text.an11.17:6.5: When they smell an odor with their nose …
translation_text.an11.17:6.6: When they taste a flavor with their tongue …
translation_text.an11.17:6.7: When they feel a touch with their body …
translation_text.an11.17:6.8: When they know an idea with their mind, they get caught up in the features and details.
translation_text.an11.17:6.9: Since the faculty of the mind is left unrestrained, bad unskillful qualities of covetousness and displeasure become overwhelming. They don’t practice restraint, they don’t protect the faculty of the mind, and they don’t achieve its restraint.
translation_text.an11.17:6.10: That’s how a mendicant doesn’t dress wounds.
translation_text.an11.17:7.1: And how does a mendicant not spread smoke?
translation_text.an11.17:7.2: It’s when a mendicant doesn’t teach others the Dhamma in detail as they learned and memorized it.
translation_text.an11.17:7.3: That’s how a mendicant doesn’t spread smoke.
translation_text.an11.17:8.1: And how does a mendicant not know the ford?
translation_text.an11.17:8.2: It’s when a mendicant doesn’t from time to time go up to those mendicants who are very learned—inheritors of the heritage, who have memorized the teachings, the monastic law, and the outlines—and ask them questions:
translation_text.an11.17:8.3: ‘Why, sir, does it say this? What does that mean?’
translation_text.an11.17:8.4: Those venerables don’t reveal what is hidden, clarify what is unclear, and dispel doubt regarding the many doubtful matters.
translation_text.an11.17:8.5: That’s how a mendicant doesn’t know the ford.
translation_text.an11.17:9.1: And how does a mendicant not know satisfaction?
translation_text.an11.17:9.2: It’s when a mendicant, when the teaching and training proclaimed by the Realized One are being taught, finds no inspiration in the meaning and the teaching, and finds no joy connected with the teaching.
translation_text.an11.17:9.3: That’s how a mendicant doesn’t know satisfaction.
translation_text.an11.17:10.1: And how does a mendicant not know the trail?
translation_text.an11.17:10.2: It’s when a mendicant doesn’t truly understand the noble eightfold path.
translation_text.an11.17:10.3: That’s how a mendicant doesn’t know the trail.
translation_text.an11.17:11.1: And how is a mendicant not skilled in pastures?
translation_text.an11.17:11.2: It’s when a mendicant doesn’t truly understand the four kinds of mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.an11.17:11.3: That’s how a mendicant is not skilled in pastures.
translation_text.an11.17:12.1: And how does a mendicant milk dry?
translation_text.an11.17:12.2: It’s when a mendicant is invited by a householder to accept robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick. But they don’t know moderation in accepting.
translation_text.an11.17:12.3: That’s how a mendicant milks dry.
translation_text.an11.17:13.1: And how does a mendicant not show extra respect to senior mendicants of long standing, long gone forth, fathers and leaders of the Saṅgha?
translation_text.an11.17:13.2: It’s when a mendicant doesn’t consistently treat senior mendicants of long standing, long gone forth, fathers and leaders of the Saṅgha with kindness by way of body, speech, and mind, both in public and in private.
translation_text.an11.17:13.3: That’s how a mendicant doesn’t show extra respect to senior mendicants of long standing, long gone forth, fathers and leaders of the Saṅgha.
translation_text.an11.17:14.1: A mendicant with these eleven qualities can’t achieve growth, improvement, or maturity in this teaching and training.
translation_text.an11.17:15.1: A cowherd with eleven factors can maintain and expand a herd of cattle.
translation_text.an11.17:15.3: It’s when a cowherd knows form, is skilled in characteristics, picks out flies’ eggs, dresses wounds, spreads smoke, knows the ford, knows satisfaction, knows the trail, is skilled in pastures, doesn’t milk dry, and shows extra respect to the bulls who are fathers and captains of the herd.
translation_text.an11.17:15.4: A cowherd with these eleven factors can maintain and expand a herd of cattle.
translation_text.an11.17:16.1: In the same way, a mendicant with eleven qualities can achieve growth, improvement, and maturity in this teaching and training.
translation_text.an11.17:16.3: It’s when a mendicant knows form, is skilled in characteristics, picks out flies’ eggs, dresses wounds, spreads smoke, knows the ford, knows satisfaction, knows the trail, is skilled in pastures, doesn’t milk dry, and shows extra respect to senior mendicants of long standing, long gone forth, fathers and leaders of the Saṅgha.
translation_text.an11.17:17.1: And how does a mendicant know form?
translation_text.an11.17:17.2: It’s when a mendicant truly understands that all form is the four principal states, or form derived from the four principal states.
translation_text.an11.17:17.3: That’s how a mendicant knows form.
translation_text.an11.17:18.1: And how is a mendicant skilled in characteristics?
translation_text.an11.17:18.2: It’s when a mendicant understands that a fool is characterized by their deeds, and an astute person is characterized by their deeds.
translation_text.an11.17:18.3: That’s how a mendicant is skilled in characteristics.
translation_text.an11.17:19.1: And how does a mendicant pick out flies’ eggs?
translation_text.an11.17:19.2: It’s when a mendicant doesn’t tolerate a sensual, malicious, or cruel thought that has arisen, but gives it up, gets rid of it, eliminates it, and exterminates it. They don’t tolerate any bad, unskillful qualities that have arisen, but give them up, get rid of them, eliminate them, and obliterate them.
translation_text.an11.17:19.3: That’s how a mendicant picks out flies’ eggs.
translation_text.an11.17:20.1: And how does a mendicant dress wounds?
translation_text.an11.17:20.2: When a mendicant sees a sight with their eyes, they don’t get caught up in the features and details.
translation_text.an11.17:20.3: If the faculty of sight were left unrestrained, bad unskillful qualities of covetousness and displeasure would become overwhelming. For this reason, they practice restraint, protecting the faculty of sight, and achieving its restraint.
translation_text.an11.17:20.4: When they hear a sound with their ears …
translation_text.an11.17:20.5: When they smell an odor with their nose …
translation_text.an11.17:20.6: When they taste a flavor with their tongue …
translation_text.an11.17:20.7: When they feel a touch with their body …
translation_text.an11.17:20.8: When they know an idea with their mind, they don’t get caught up in the features and details.
translation_text.an11.17:20.9: If the faculty of mind were left unrestrained, bad unskillful qualities of covetousness and displeasure would become overwhelming. For this reason, they practice restraint, protecting the faculty of mind, and achieving its restraint.
translation_text.an11.17:20.10: That’s how a mendicant dresses wounds.
translation_text.an11.17:21.1: And how does a mendicant spread smoke?
translation_text.an11.17:21.2: It’s when a mendicant teaches others the Dhamma in detail as they learned and memorized it.
translation_text.an11.17:21.3: That’s how a mendicant spreads smoke.
translation_text.an11.17:22.1: And how does a mendicant know the ford?
translation_text.an11.17:22.2: It’s when from time to time a mendicant goes up to those mendicants who are very learned—inheritors of the heritage, who have memorized the teachings, the monastic law, and the outlines—and asks them questions:
translation_text.an11.17:22.3: ‘Why, sir, does it say this? What does that mean?’
translation_text.an11.17:22.4: Those venerables reveal what is hidden, clarify what is unclear, and dispel doubt regarding the many doubtful matters.
translation_text.an11.17:22.5: That’s how a mendicant knows the ford.
translation_text.an11.17:23.1: And how does a mendicant know satisfaction?
translation_text.an11.17:23.2: It’s when a mendicant, when the teaching and training proclaimed by the Realized One are being taught, finds inspiration in the meaning and the teaching, and finds joy connected with the teaching.
translation_text.an11.17:23.3: That’s how a mendicant knows satisfaction.
translation_text.an11.17:24.1: And how does a mendicant know the trail?
translation_text.an11.17:24.2: It’s when a mendicant truly understands the noble eightfold path.
translation_text.an11.17:24.3: That’s how a mendicant knows the trail.
translation_text.an11.17:25.1: And how is a mendicant skilled in pastures?
translation_text.an11.17:25.2: It’s when a mendicant truly understands the four kinds of mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.an11.17:25.3: That’s how a mendicant is skilled in pastures.
translation_text.an11.17:26.1: And how does a mendicant not milk dry?
translation_text.an11.17:26.2: It’s when a mendicant is invited by a householder to accept robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick.
translation_text.an11.17:26.3: And that mendicant knows moderation in accepting.
translation_text.an11.17:26.4: That’s how a mendicant doesn’t milk dry.
translation_text.an11.17:27.1: And how does a mendicant show extra respect to senior mendicants of long standing, long gone forth, fathers and leaders of the Saṅgha?
translation_text.an11.17:27.2: It’s when a mendicant consistently treats senior mendicants of long standing, long gone forth, fathers and leaders of the Saṅgha with kindness by way of body, speech, and mind, both in public and in private.
translation_text.an11.17:27.3: That’s how a mendicant shows extra respect to senior mendicants of long standing, long gone forth, fathers and leaders of the Saṅgha.
translation_text.an11.17:28.1: A mendicant with these eleven qualities can achieve growth, improvement, or maturity in this teaching and training.”
